title: Sith Intelligence
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Citation}}

{{Quote|The entire point of Sith Intelligence is for it to be overseen by the Sith.|Darth Marr to Cipher Nine concerning Lana Beniko's appointment as Minister of Sith Intelligence|<ref name="The Enemy Within" />}}
'''Sith Intelligence''' was the successor of the [[Imperial Intelligence (Sith Empire)|Imperial Intelligence]] of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]].

==History==
{{Quote|I may be out of the game, but you're not. I hear rumors Intelligence is on the verge of reforming at last.|The last Minister of Intelligence, to the former Cipher Nine on Rishi|<ref name="Forfeit">{{TORcite|exp=shadow|type=class|Imperial Agent|Forfeit|Rishi}}</ref>}}
Following the near collapse of [[Imperial Intelligence (Sith Empire)|Imperial Intelligence]] at the hands of the [[Star Cabal]] during the [[Galactic War]], the [[Dark Council]] took the opportunity to dissolve the weakened organization permanently. In place of the old department, they planned to form a new Sith Intelligence to operate as the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire's]] new intelligence arm. Unlike its predecessor, which maintained a degree of independence from its Sith overlords, Sith Intelligence was to be overseen directly by the Sith.<ref name="The Star Chamber" />

Following a battle on the moon [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] against the [[Order of Revan|Revanites]], [[Darth Marr]] placed [[Lana Beniko]] in charge of Sith Intelligence.<ref name="The Enemy Within" />

Sith Intelligence's operations moved to [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], where the former Sith Emperor, [[Tenebrae|Vitiate]], began possessing combatants on both sides during the Republic's [[Invasion of Ziost|invasion]] of the planet. The organization was later left in disarray following Vitiate consuming all life on the planet. Minister Beniko planned on restoring Sith Intelligence by making their operations mobile. However, whatever progress was being made was lost in the chaos that followed the hitherto-unknown [[Eternal Empire conquest|Eternal Empire's assault]] on the galaxy at large.

By [[3631 BBY]],<ref>{{TOR_updates|4.0}}</ref> Beniko was no longer affiliated with Sith Intelligence but knew most its assets were destroyed in the previous fighting with the [[Eternal Empire]]. She believed [[Sith Emperor/Legends|Empress]] [[Darth Acina|Acina]], who had since ascended as head of the Sith Empire, preferred to rely on technological surveillance and did not know if any attempts at reconstructing the organization had been made.<ref>{{TORcite|exp=kotfe|chapter=4}}</ref>

===Order of Zildrog campaign===
{{Quote|I've discovered a dangerous rogue faction within Sith Intelligence, led by a former Imperial Cipher and a powerful Sith Lord. Major Korven and Lord Anril are launching devastating attacks on Alliance settlements. By striking us, they're attempting to reclaim the glory days of the Sith Empire. We've tracked their operations to Vanguard Station. Board the facility and end this threat.|Lana Beniko briefs the Alliance strike team upon arrival at Vanguard Station|<ref name="Fractured">{{TORcite|exp=kotet|type=uprising|Fractured}}</ref>}}
[[File:Vanguard_Station.png|thumb|left|250px|Sith Intelligence defectors hijacked Vanguard Station (pictured) to attack outposts of the newly formed Eternal Alliance.]]
Following the collapse of the Eternal Empire and formation of the [[Eternal Alliance]] in [[3630 BBY]],<ref>{{TOR_updates|5.0}}</ref> a rogue faction of Sith Intelligence defectors led by [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anril]] and former Imperial Intelligence [[Cipher Agent|cipher agent]] [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Korven]] hijacked [[Vanguard Station]] and began attacking vulnerable Alliance settlements. An elite Alliance strike team [[Battle of Vanguard Station|infiltrated]] the station and defeated the defectors' leaders, putting an end to their attacks.<ref name="Fractured" /> This incident was one of five [[Uprisings against the Eternal Alliance|uprisings]] concurrently directed against the newly formed Alliance, causing [[Alliance Intelligence (Eternal Alliance)|Alliance Intelligence]] to theorize that a single unknown party may have been behind the attacks.<ref name="AIR1">{{TORcite|type=codex|Alliance Intelligence Report: A Cause for Concern}}</ref> Shortly thereafter, when five additional uprisings were sparked against the Alliance, Alliance Intelligence confirmed that a single entity was responsible for initiating all the uprisings and considered Sith Intelligence as a possible suspect.<ref name="AIR2">{{TORcite|type=codex|Alliance Intelligence Report: A Common Thread}}</ref>

In the midst of the [[War on Iokath|Battle of Iokath]] in [[3629 BBY]],<ref>{{TOR_updates|5.2}}</ref> Beniko conveyed her concerns to the [[Alliance Commander]] that an unknown party was responsible for the clash of Republic, Imperial, and Alliance forces converging at [[Iokath]] simultaneously. Alliance operations leader and former [[Republic Strategic Information Service]] agent [[Theron Shan]] suggested Sith Intelligence as a possible suspect.<ref>{{TORcite|exp=kotet|type=general|Showdown on Iokath|Iokath}}</ref> The real culprit was later revealed to be the [[Order of Zildrog]].<ref name="The Nathema Conspiracy">{{TORcite|exp=kotet|type=general|The Nathema Conspiracy|Odessen}}</ref>

===Third Galactic War===
During the Empire's [[Invasion of Ossus|invasion]] of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] in [[3628 BBY]],<ref>{{TOR_updates|5.10.0}}</ref> two Sith Intelligence assets, both [[Nautolan/Legends|Nautolan]] females, reported to a Sith Lord on the condition of the planet's [[Jedi Colony|Jedi colony]] and advised on weaknesses to be exploited.<ref>{{TORcite|exp=kotet|type=weekly|Daily Area: Ossus|Ossus}}</ref><ref>{{TORcite|exp=kotet|type=weekly|Heroic Missions: Ossus|Ossus}}</ref>

The [[3627 BBY|following year]],<ref>{{TOR_updates|5.10.3}}</ref> Sith Intelligence arranged for the [[Nova Blades]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] gang to begin conducting periodic [[Dantooine Incursion|attacks]] on the Republic [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural world]] of [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. The planet was a key and heavily defended staging area for Republic incursions into Imperial space. Darth Xarion recruited the pirates to attack the planet from within Republic territory. The incursion created a chaotic situation in which the Empire was able covertly deploy a strike team to the planet and establish a hidden base.<ref>{{TORcite|type=faction|Imperial|The Marauders of Dantooine|Imperial Fleet}}</ref>

Following the [[Battle of the Meridian Complex|Battle of Corellia]] months later,<ref>{{TOR_updates|6.0}}</ref> Sith Intelligence sought to influence the [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] government but required comprehensive intelligence about the ruling class in [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]], the planetary [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city. By this time, spies and inside sources had proven valuable resources, but additional surveillance was required. Accordingly, Imperial heroes installed half-a-dozen surveillance devices around Iziz's perimeter.<ref>{{TORcite|exp=ons|type=daily|Royal Reconnaissance|Onderon}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|There's already talk of your place in the new world of Sith Intelligence—head of a slate of black projects, with the conspirators' secrets as your weapons.|The last Minister of Intelligence informs Cipher Nine of Imperial Intelligence's replacement with Sith Intelligence following the dismantling of the Star Cabal|<ref name="The Star Chamber" />}}
Sith Intelligence was first mentioned at the conclusion of the [[Imperial Agent]] class story in the [[BioWare]]-[[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' in [[2011]]. Regardless of players choices, the former [[Keeper (Imperial Intelligence)|Minister of Intelligence]] tells [[Cipher Nine]] that the Dark Council is planning to form a new Sith Intelligence, rather than rebuild Imperial Intelligence. Should the player hand the [[Black Codex]] over to the Dark Council, the Minister explains that there will be a place in the new intelligence agency for Cipher Nine, likely headed by people like him/her. Should the player keep the Black Codex then the they are given the option to either use it to flee and work as an invisible agent of the Empire, or to remain in the Empire and "make it a better place". Finally, should the player choose to hand over the codex to [[Ardun Kothe]], then the Minister tells Cipher Nine that the Empire will likely perceive him/her as a renegade.<ref name="The Star Chamber" />

Additionally, if the player is an Imperial Agent in the [[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Fallen Empire|''Knights of the Fallen Empire'' expansion]], shortly after landing in the [[Endless Swamp]], Lana Beniko will reveal what she knows of the fate of Sith Intelligence.
